prompt:
A wide half-body shot of two young people standing at the railing of an open-air rooftop club bar, authentic daily life photography, natural candid moment. She leans back lightly against him — both looking out at the city lights below, drinks in hand, a comfortable weight to the silence between them.
East Asian young woman in her early 20s. Almond-shaped eyes with natural double eyelids, slightly elongated eye corners, expression soft and open — the kind of face that appears when the music is behind you and the city is in front. Straight refined nose with a delicate bridge. Skin tone fair to light beige (NC10–NC20). Skin subsurface scattering visible under warm rooftop bar light mixed with distant city glow, specular micro-highlights on her bare shoulders and collarbone, fine foundation powder grain perceptible. She wears a rose-gold satin slip dress on thin straps — the fabric catching the city light, hem above the knee, moving faintly in the night breeze. He stands close behind her, hands loosely on the railing beside hers. Two or three stray hairs lift and cross her cheek displaced by the rooftop breeze, natural unplanned imperfection, not geometrically symmetrical.
Mixed artificial light sources — warm amber rooftop bar lanterns cast a soft glow across both figures from behind. The vast city light grid spreads below and behind them, bokeh points of orange and white light filling the distance. A light wind is implied in the fabric movement and lifted hair. The mood is gentle and warm — quiet contentment, a small almost-smile, sense of stillness above the city. Subtle ISO 400 film grain in shadow areas, photographic noise texture not CG render smoothness. Aspect ratio 3:2. No watermark, no text overlay, not cartoon, not digitally painted, not illustration, not anime.
